purpose: Step-by-step guide for connecting Synapse to a new project. Walks through pointing Synapse at the project (3 options: store path, CWD, --project), running !initialize (8-step process: verify prerequisites, detect tech stack, create .synapse/, scaffold CLAUDE.md, generate TOC, init dashboards, start server, report), reviewing CLAUDE.md, generating TOC, bootstrapping PKI with !learn, project-specific commands, disconnecting projects, and troubleshooting common setup issues.
gotchas: !initialize is idempotent -- safe to re-run, skips existing files
--skip-toc and --skip-claude flags available for partial init
Dashboards are swarm-specific not project-specific -- changing project doesn't affect existing dashboards
patterns: Tech stack detection via config file presence (package.json, tsconfig.json, etc.)
Monorepo detection: npm/pnpm workspaces, lerna, nx, turbo, cargo, go
conventions: Project root resolution priority: --project > stored config > CWD
.synapse/config.json links project to tracker_root with metadata
TOC generation parallelized via agent swarm
